paper_title: An Approach with Olive Varieties and Dual-Successive-Screen Model in Olive Trees

methods.description: This method involves studying the S-alleles of olive varieties to calculate the proportion of compatible and incompatible pollen, which helps in understanding pollination success and self-incompatibility in olive orchards.

steps.description: Study the S-alleles of the varieties to establish relationships between the G groups (G1 or G2) and the S-allele groups attributed to each variety in PASI.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze S-alleles to determine pollen compatibility

steps.description: Check whether DSI and PASI could work independently by reporting data for G1 and G2 and adding the PASI pair.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze independence of DSI and PASI

methods.description: This method involves diagnosing self-incompatibility (SI) in olive trees by genotyping and analyzing cross data, considering the possibility of selfing and the role of modifier loci.

steps.description: Genotype the host tree, the father tree, and the embryos to determine which comes from non-self-pollen.
Introduce a quantitative threshold to diagnose SI from cross data, accepting that crossing two incompatible varieties may lead to some fruit.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze cross data with quantitative threshold for SI

steps.description: Consider the possibility of selfing and the role of modifier loci in self-fertilization.
steps.searchable_summary: evaluate selfing and modifier loci impact
